#include <vector>
#include <cmath>
using namespace std;

class Solution {
public:
    int kthFactor(int n, int k) {
        vector<int> factors;
        for (int i = 1; i * i <= n; ++i) {
            if (n % i == 0) {
                factors.push_back(i);
                if (i != n / i) {
                    factors.push_back(n / i);
                }
            }
        }
        sort(factors.begin(), factors.end());
        if (k <= factors.size()) {
            return factors[k - 1];
        } else {
            return -1;
        }
    }
};

class Solution {
public:
    int kthFactor(int n, int k) {
        vector<int> lower, upper;
        for (int i = 1; i * i <= n; ++i) {
            if (n % i == 0) {
                lower.push_back(i);
                if (i != n / i) {
                    upper.push_back(n / i);
                }
            }
        }
        vector<int> factors;
        // Add lower factors
        for (int i = 0; i < lower.size(); ++i) {
            factors.push_back(lower[i]);
        }
        // Add upper factors in reverse order
        for (int i = upper.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
            factors.push_back(upper[i]);
        }
        if (k <= factors.size()) {
            return factors[k - 1];
        } else {
            return -1;
        }
    }
};

solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int kthFactor(int n, int k) {
        std::vector<int> lower, upper;
        for (int i = 1; i * i <= n; ++i) {
            if (n % i == 0) {
                lower.push_back(i);
                if (i != n / i) {
                    upper.push_back(n / i);
                }
            }
        }
        std::vector<int> factors;
        for (int i = 0; i < lower.size(); ++i) {
            factors.push_back(lower[i]);
        }
        for (int i = upper.size() - 1; i >= 0; --i) {
            factors.push_back(upper[i]);
        }
        if (k <= factors.size()) {
            return factors[k - 1];
        } else {
            return -1;
        }
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(√n)
space complexity: O(m) where m is the number of factors of n
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution efficiently generates factors by iterating up to √n, collecting pairs of factors. Lower factors are stored in ascending order, and upper factors are added in reverse order to maintain sorted output without explicit sorting. This approach reduces both time and space complexity compared to brute-force methods.
